body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule J 2025 tax credit for home-support services for seniors.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.J-V%282025-12%29.pdf
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.G-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule J numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 600
Value: 1200
Value: 5
Value: 0.05
Value: 39
Value: 0.39
Value: 71010
Value: 19500
Value: 39000
Value: 25500
Value: 45000
Value: 51000
Value: 7605
Value: 15210
Value: 9945
Value: 17550
Value: 19890

Schedule J calculates the tax credit for home-support services for seniors and carries the final amount to TP-1 line 458. Line 24 adds lines 22 and 23 from form TP-1029.61.MD-V for private seniors' residence eligible services. Lines 30 through 33 calculate rent-related services by limiting monthly rent to a minimum of $600 and a maximum of $1,200, multiplying the limited rent by 5%, and multiplying by the number of months rent was paid. Line 34 adds the two line 33 rent-service columns. Line 36 enters eligible condominium services. Lines 50, 52, 54 and 56 enter eligible direct home-support service costs, and line 74 adds those direct service costs.

Line 75 adds line 24, line 34, line 36 and line 74, subject to the annual expense limit in the TP-1 guide for line 458. The guide gives a $19,500 annual expense limit and $7,605 maximum credit for a single non-dependent senior, a $39,000 annual expense limit and $15,210 maximum credit for a couple of non-dependent seniors, a $25,500 annual expense limit and $9,945 maximum credit for a single dependent senior, a $45,000 annual expense limit and $17,550 maximum credit for a couple with one dependent senior and one non-dependent senior, and a $51,000 annual expense limit and $19,890 maximum credit for a couple of dependent seniors.

Lines 80 through 82 calculate family income by adding the taxpayer's and spouse's net income from line 275. Lines 84 through 87 carry the eligible costs to the credit calculation. Line 88 multiplies eligible costs by 39%. Line 89 requires Work Chart 458 to calculate a reduction if family income on line 82 is more than $71,010. Line 90 subtracts the line 89 reduction from line 88 and carries the result to TP-1 line 458, floored at zero in the encoded rule.
